Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Continued To Jan-19-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Continued To Dec-17-15 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER S. FORGIE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CHARLES D. NAYLOR, TEL. NO. 1-310-514-1200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JENNIFER M. PORTER OF THE LAW OFFICES OF KEESAL YOUNG & LOGAN, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-0136 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PATRICK DAVIS AND F/V SUSAN T. INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES is denied. Plaintiff characterizes the injury as purely latent. The time of event rule applies to patent injuries or where the plaintiff has sustained both immediate and latent injuries. The discovery rule applies to purely latent injuries. "A pure latent injury case is one in which 'the plaintiff fails to discover either the injury or its cause until long after the negligent act occurred.'" Clay v. Union Carbide Corp., 828 F.2d 1103, 1106 (5th Cir. 1987). At par. 7 of plaintiff's declaration, he explains that he did not experience any discomfort until a week after the incident. If the statute of limitations commenced at that time, then the claims are timely. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff did or should have discerned injury on June 29, 2011, and whether the discovery rule applies. Therefore, there is a triable issue of material fact whether the 3-year statute of limitations bars the Jones Act and unseaworthiness claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
=302/eg

Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Continued To Feb-04-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER S. FORGIE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CHARLES D. NAYLOR, TEL. NO. (310) 514-1200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PATRICK DAVIS, F/V SUSAN T. INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Complaint sufficiently alleges that Plaintiff did not become aware of any injury until August 11, 2011. The cases upon which Defendants rely for the application of the "time of event rule" are all distinguishable in that each of those plaintiffs had identified some injury but delayed filing actions due to limited knowledge of the extent or cause of their injuries. Here, application of the discovery rule is appropriate because absent any knowledge of injury, Plaintiff could not have been on notice that he may have had a claim against the Defendants. However, the Complaint does not state sufficient facts for Plaintiff to rely on the discovery rule to delay the accrual of his claims. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 809.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
